rationale:
Regex-correctness/monotonicity/no-false-positive lens, all pass. (1) Rejects bare `$`: node matrix confirms `/[\x60;|&
$]/.test` is true for `$HOME`, `${x}`, `$x` — so validateShellSafe now throws on variable-expansion. (2) MONOTONE: brute-forced all 128 ASCII code points plus the `$(` digraph against old `/[\x60;|&
]|\$\(/` — zero cases the old regex rejected that the new one accepts; the prior class members backtick/`;`/`|`/`&`/`
` are retained verbatim and `$(` is subsumed because it contains `$`. (3) Char class well-formed: `$` inside `[...]` is a literal (not an end-anchor); node parses and matches it correctly. (4) NO over-rejection: `p113`, `113-panel-verb-workflow-followups`, `bead/mindspec-x.1`, `spec/113-...`, `mindspec-r6hk.2` all pass (no `$` in repo slug/spec/target/bead conventions). Routing confirmed: validateArgs (L455) runs before every buildCommand callsite (465/518/532) and calls validateShellSafe on slug/spec/bead_id/target; round is an integer, mix.family is enum-checked — no `$`-bearing value reaches the shell string. Tightening applied where it matters.

findings.title: Bare `$` correctly rejected
findings.detail: /[\x60;|&\n$]/.test returns true for $HOME, ${x}, $x, and $(x); validateShellSafe throws on all. Bare `$` alone also matches (true).

findings.title: Monotone tightening verified
findings.detail: Brute-force over all 128 ASCII chars + the $( digraph: 0 cases where old regex rejected but new accepts. No regression; strict superset of rejected inputs.

findings.title: Char class well-formed, no false positives
findings.detail: $ inside [...] is a literal, not an anchor. All real repo conventions (p113, 113-panel-verb-workflow-followups, bead/mindspec-x.1, spec/113-..., mindspec-r6hk.2) pass. No legit slug/spec/target/bead ID contains $.
